preamble: In many Japanese dialects, including Standard Japanese (traditionally), the voiced velar plosive [g] is realized as a velar nasal [ŋ] when it occurs in intervocalic positions or word-medially.
sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Tsujimura, N. (2013). An Introduction to Japanese Linguistics.
